title: Country Gravy - Sausage
description: Creamy, peppery sausage gravy loaded with flavor and built for biscuits. Thick, rich, and exactly what you want when you are not pretending to eat light.
ingredients: 8 oz breakfast sausage links, casings removed and meat broken up
Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Melt butter in a large skillet over medium heat. Add sausage and cook, breaking it into small pieces, until browned, about 10 minutes.
Add scallions and cook until softened, about 3 minutes.
Stir in flour a little at a time until fully combined and the mixture becomes pasty. Cook for 2 to 3 minutes to remove the raw flour taste.
Slowly whisk in milk, about 1 cup at a time, until smooth and combined.
Continue whisking as the gravy thickens, about 5 minutes.
Increase heat slightly to bring to a gentle simmer, then season with cayenne, salt, and black pepper.
Reduce heat to medium-low and let simmer for 10 to 15 minutes, stirring occasionally, until thick and fully developed.
Garnish with extra scallions and a pinch of cayenne before serving.
notes: Keep whisking when adding milk to avoid lumps.
If it gets too thick, add a splash of milk to loosen it up.
Best served immediately over biscuits or toast.
whyThisHits: It is rich, fast, and hits exactly where it should. This is the kind of gravy that turns a basic breakfast into something worth sitting down for.
pairing: Serve over warm biscuits, toast, or even fried chicken if you want to go all in.
footerLine: Art for Your Mouth. Because boring food is a waste of time.
